no-privelege: You do not have permission to set this to an Admin stall.
not-enough-stock: Not enough items left in stock for this purchase.
no-price: This item does not yet have a price
no-product: There is nothing for sale for this slot
no-register-space: There is not enough space in the Shop's Register to recieve payment
no-stall-space: There is not enough space in the Shop's Stall to recieve goods
purchased-zero-quantity: You must purchase atleast 1 item at a time.
not-enough-money: Not enough items in your hand to purchase this item.
not-enough-capacity: Not enough container capacity to purchase this item.
not-registered-with-shop: This stall is not registered with a shop yet. Unable to make a purchase.
couldnt-find-register: Unable to find the register block for shop - is it in an unloaded chunk?
item-cost: This item costs {0}x {1}. Please have payment in your hand.
gui-couldnt-read-response: The requested Archive Address could not be read.
gui-error-parsing: There was a problem loading RichText. Perhaps there is a problem with the source document
gui-error-tell-the-dev: There was a problem loading - Please forward this to the developer so he can fix it:
gui-no-sales: There are no sales this month.
gui-no-shops: There are currently no shops available to view in the catalog
gui-whitelisted-shops-note: Leave blank for all stalls owned by you
no-gacha-ball: No gacha ball provided in input slot
gacha-atleast-one: You need to bundle a gacha ball with atleast one item
gacha-bundled-zero: You must bundle atleast one gacha ball
ledger-already-set: This ledger already recording sales for a shop
ledger-shop-not-found: The Shop associated with this ledger no longer exists
ledger-not-set: This ledger is not set to any shop. Sneak-Right-Click a register to link it
ledger-set: Ledger set to retrieve sales from {0}
itemdesc-book-tenretni*: This book seems to magically transcribe texts from something called the Tenretni Archive, a complex network of machines and databanks
tooltip-price: Place the item you wish to use as the price for the product here
tooltip-product: This represents the item stack that the customer will purchase
tooltip-cost-per-purchase: The number of items the customer must pay to recieve the product
tooltip-items-per-purchase: The number of product items returned to the custer for each purchase
tooltip-crate-cost-per-purchase: The number of items the stall should purchase from the customer for them to recieve the currency
tooltip-crate-items-per-purchase: The number of items the customer will recieve for selling the product to the stall
tooltip-decoration-block: Place a chiseled block in this slot to set a custom model for this stall
tooltip-admin-shop: Enable/Disable this stall as an Admin Shop. If enabled, Product is infinite and linked Registers are optional. In the case that a Shop Register is not set, any currency collected will be deleted instead of placed inside the Register
tooltip-shop: Select which shop this stall belongs to. Any currency collected will be placed inside the register
tooltip-fuzzy-matching: Ignores all attributes on a given item and only cares about the item type. Example: Any Lantern instead of a Copper Lantern or Bronze Lantern.
tooltip-limit-purchases: Should there be a set number of purchase until this stall stops accepting any more trades
tooltip-discard-product: Should the purchased stock be deleted instead of placed inside the Purchased Stock slots
tooltip-register-fallback: Should the stall pull additional currency from inside the linked Register if the Currency slots become empty
tooltip-purchased-stock: The desired item this stall will purchase from customers
tooltip-purchased-stock-output: Any stock purchased from customers will be placed inside these slots
tooltip-returned-currency: The currency item returned to the customer in exchange for the purchased stock
tooltip-returned-currency-output: Currency needed to purchase items from customers should be placed inside these slots. The currency item should be set via the 'Currency' slot on the left.
tooltip-total-count-randomizer: Enable/Disable Winning Chance based on the total number of items in all slots, instead of the number of stacks
tooltip-items-per-gacha: The selection of Items and its respective amounts that should be placed in each Gacha Ball
tooltip-gacha-name: The item name of the outputted Gacha Ball
tooltip-gacha-input: Place empty Gacha Balls to be filled by the machine here
tooltip-gacha-output: Bundled Gacha Balls will be outputted here once filled with items
tooltip-serving-transfer: Transfer meal servings to/from the Meal Container. Supports Cooking Pots and Crocks.
tooltip-purchases-left: If Limited Purchases is enabled, how many more sales can be made before this stall stops trading entirely. For every purchase, this number will be decremented by 1 until it reaches 0, and no more purchase can be made and the number must be set to a number greater than 0 to resume trading
tooltip-teller-conversion: Place two items that would be considered equivalent in terms of currency. This trade is bidirectional - Customers can obtain the right handed item in exchange for the left, or vice versa obtain the left handed item in exchange for the right. E.g. 1 Gold Bar can be traded for 5 Silver Bars, and 5 Silver Bars can be traded for 1 Gold Bar
tooltip-whitelisted-shops: Which shops should the coupon be valid for. If no shops are selected, the coupon will be valid for all shops owned by you
tooltip-coupon: Sets the properties of the coupon. You can specify whether the coupon gives a discount or additional product with either a flat rate or a percentage
tooltip-consume-coupon: Whether or not the coupon will be used up in the trade. If unchecked, the coupon can be used repeatedly
tooltip-item-whitelist: Specifies which items a coupon is valid for. Leave blank for all trades
tooltip-blacklist: Specifies whether or not the items above are excluded instead of included
tooltip-paper: Place a piece of Parchment here to cut up into coupons
tooltip-short-description: Provide a short description of the shop that should be displayed to users browsing the catalog
tooltip-stall-access: Whether or not players should also have access to your stalls as well
tooltip-webhook: Specify a web address that should recieve a snapshot of products purchased from your stalls. Typically this would be a Discord Webhook URL
tooltip-shop-access: Which players should have access to your register in order to collect/deposit funds
tooltip-trade-pass: Which item, if any, should be required in order to purchase items from your stalls
tooltip-description: Provide a detailed description of the shop that should be displayed to users when they view your shop directly via the catalog
tooltip-waypoint-visible: If enabled, the waypoint is broadcasted to all other players to let them know where your shop is located. The location is also shown in the shop catalog and customers can use the Location Link to view your shop on the map
tooltip-currency: Any currency collected from your shops will appear here. This is also used by the Teller Machine to exchange currencies. If this section is full then no more trades can be conducted as the payment would be unable to get collected
tooltip-coupons: Any coupons collected from your shops will appear here. If this section is full, no more coupons can be collected
handbooktext-register: Allows you to set up your own personal shop. Any proceeds from a given mat, crate, clothing stall, or sculpture stall linked to this shop will go directly to this register's inventory. Be sure to empty out the register from time to time, as you will not be able to make any sales if it is full. If you want to keep track of all sales for your shop, you can craft a ledger and link it to this stall in order to view the sales on a Year/Month basis. The Register can also create a global, publicly visible waypoint by enabling the tick box in the Register's menu. If enabled, your register's location will be broadcasted so other players can find your shop easier.
handbooktext-tellermachine: Allows your customers to exchange currency between two types. The currency conversion is done directly from your register's inventory, so it may only happen if you have enough items to support the trade. Note that these trades are bidirectional, so do not set up conversions which you do not consider equivalent. As an example 10 Copper Currency might be worth 1 Silver Currency. Such a trade would allow players to exchange either 10 Copper for 1 Silver, or 1 Silver for 10 Copper.
handbooktext-stall: Allows you to sell a number of items to players for purchase. In order to function, it must be linked to a register in order to function properly. Any goods sold by this stall will have its proceeds delievered straight to the register's inventory.
handbooktext-gacha: Allows you to sell chance-based goods to players for purchase. The gacha machine must be filled with gacha balls which can be filled in a gacha loader. In order to function, it must be linked to a register in order to function properly. Any goods sold by this stall will have its proceeds delievered straight to the register's inventory.
handbooktext-gachaloader: Allows you to fill gacha balls with items to be used in a gacha stall. Each ball can be filled with up to 6 different stacks of items.
handbooktext-clothingstand: Allows you to sell a number of clothing items to players for purchase. In order to function, it must be linked to a register in order to function properly. Any goods sold by this stall will have its proceeds delievered straight to the register's inventory.
handbooktext-weaponrack: Allows you to sell a up to two types of weapons or tools and a shield to players for purchase. In order to function, it must be linked to a register in order to function properly. Any goods sold by this stall will have its proceeds delievered straight to the register's inventory.
handbooktext-toolrack: Allows you to sell a number of tools to players for purchase. In order to function, it must be linked to a register in order to function properly. Any goods sold by this stall will have its proceeds delievered straight to the register's inventory.
handbooktext-tenretni: Allows you to read third-party data from the internet (Right now, just Pastebin.com) by providing its URL ID. Any updates to the Paste will automatically update upon re-opening the book
handbooktext-purchasecrate: Allows you to purchase items from other players, instead of selling items. Useful for purchasing items to supplly your profession, such as a Blacksmith purchasing metal nuggets and then selling the refined ingots in at a shop map. This shop behaves differently to other shop stalls, as it has dedicated storage for the purchased stock rather than despositing it into the register. This prevents the register from filling up entirely with purchased goods and preventing trades from other stalls - especially with bulk amounts where multiple stacks are purchased at a time.
